products.menuLine: Booker's Bourbon · Kentucky · $85 · 125 proof (62.5% ABV) · Journey: Advanced · Notes: caramel, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Experienced bourbon drinker, bold cigar, wants proof.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants cask-strength bourbon for a maduro — caramel, oak, and serious heat.
guidance.memberBlurb: Booker's — uncut Kentucky strength with caramel and peanut brittle over oak. Built for a full-bodied cigar when you want the glass to match the stick.

products.menuLine: Buffalo Trace Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $28 · 90 proof (45% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: vanilla, caramel

guidance.bestFor: Bourbon beginner, first pour, user wants approachable sweetness without heat.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants peat, high-proof spice bomb, or allocated wheated collector only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member is new to bourbon or asks what to start with — vanilla, caramel, gentle oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Buffalo Trace — vanilla, mint, and molasses over brown sugar, toffee, and dark fruit. The distillery's official teaching pour for bourbon newcomers.

products.menuLine: Bulleit Kentucky Straight Bourbon · Kentucky · $32 · 90 proof (45.0% ABV) · Journey: Novice · Notes: dried fruit, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: High-rye curious, classic cocktail, recognizable shelf pour.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend as approachable high-rye bourbon — dried fruit and spice without Booker's heat.
guidance.memberBlurb: Bulleit Bourbon — high-rye spice and dried fruit in a pour every bar stocks. Easy to recommend when someone just says bourbon.

products.menuLine: Casa Dragones Joven · Jalisco, Mexico · $285 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: citrus, pepper

guidance.bestFor: Ultra-luxury tequila, celebration, member names Casa Dragones.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants top-shelf tequila beyond 1942 — crisp, floral, luxury agave.
guidance.memberBlurb: Casa Dragones Joven — crisp luxury agave with floral lift. The tequila pour for members who already own Clase Azul and want what insiders drink.

products.menuLine: Casamigos Reposado · Jalisco, Mexico · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, caramel

guidance.bestFor: Celebrity tequila ask, lounge demo, gift pour.
guidance.avoidIf: Agave purist who rejects sweet profiles.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member names Casamigos — vanilla, caramel, and easy sweetness.
guidance.memberBlurb: Casamigos Reposado — vanilla and caramel agave in the bottle everyone saw on Instagram. Have a good answer ready.

products.menuLine: Chivas Regal 12 Year · Scotland · $38 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, apple

guidance.bestFor: Blend beginner, mild cigar, classic brand ask.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants an easy blended Scotch — honey, apple, and hazelnut.
guidance.memberBlurb: Chivas 12 — honey and apple in a blend your father probably drank. Approachable prestige that still works beside a cigar.

products.menuLine: Clase Azul Reposado · Jalisco, Mexico · $150 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, caramel

guidance.bestFor: Tequila prestige, gift pour, member wants recognizable luxury agave.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants blanco-only or mezcal smoke.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when visual luxury and tequila recognition matter — vanilla, caramel, iconic ceramic.
guidance.memberBlurb: Clase Azul Reposado — vanilla and caramel agave in the ceramic bottle everyone photographs. Luxury tequila as theater.

products.menuLine: Courvoisier VSOP Exclusif · Cognac, France · $45 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: apricot, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: VSOP cognac, classic brand, after-dinner cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when approachable cognac is needed — apricot, vanilla, and oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Courvoisier VSOP — apricot and vanilla in a classic French VSOP. Cognac entry before you reach for Hennessy XO.
